extraction.one_line_summary: HIRSCHI attacked with 50m remaining on Mur de Huy to overtake WOODS who had gone at 200m, first Swiss winner since 1952.
tactical_patterns: VANSEVENANT in early break reveals Deceuninck testing young riders in strategic race situations despite inexperience.
URÁN bridging solo from peloton on penultimate climb suggests targeting positioning before final Mur, not contesting sprint.
POLANC leadout for POGAČAR at Mur base indicates UAE prioritized positioning over firepower despite leader's climbing ability.
HIRSCHI waiting until 50m versus WOODS at 200m proves extreme patience beats early acceleration even on steep finales.

rider_signals.HIRSCHI Marc: positive: timing sprint launch at 50m rather than following standard 200m attacks reveals exceptional tactical maturity
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: negative: going at 200m with strong acceleration insufficient to hold off late movers on Mur de Huy
rider_signals.VANSEVENANT Mauri: positive: surviving as last breakaway rider and holding 15s on URÁN shows climbing promise despite crash
rider_signals.URÁN Rigoberto: neutral: solo bridge attempt on penultimate climb showed positioning ambition but caught immediately at Mur base
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: negative: requiring POLANC leadout and finishing ninth suggests Mur de Huy's steep explosive gradient unsuited to sustained power
rider_signals.COSNEFROY Benoît: positive: second place without mentioned attacks indicates excellent positioning discipline and late-race punch
rider_signals.POELS Wout: neutral: attacking first among favorites but finishing eighth shows effort mistimed or insufficient power reserve
